title: Mediator-class battle cruiser
content:

The '''''Mediator''-class battle cruiser''' was an advanced [[Warship/Legends|warship]] developed by the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] for use by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|Defense Fleet]].<ref name="VP" />

==Characteristics==
The ''Mediator''-class was over half the size of the prototype [[Viscount-class Star Defender|''Viscount''-class Star Defender]] [[Viscount (Viscount-class)|''Viscount'']] and was a successor to the previous generations of Star Cruisers, including the [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 ''Home One'' type]], itself at one point the largest warship in the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel Fleet]].<ref name="TCSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

Like previous Mon Calamari cruisers, they were heavily but conventionally armed with [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] and [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], defensive [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] and heavy armor, [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] projectors, and [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. Little is known about their overall layout, but one of the ship's main hangar bays had an opening on the ventral side of the hull.<ref name="VP" />

The armor inside the ship was strong enough to prevent complete destruction of the vessel even in the case of a nuclear detonation inside a hangar.<ref name="VP" /> This was in sharp contrast to [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] designs like the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]], which could be destroyed with a similar charge placed in a hangar.<ref name="Xw">[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]</ref>

==History==
The ''Mediator''-class ships were designed after the [[Black Fleet Crisis]] as more powerful successors to the previous classes of [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Star Cruisers]] built by the [[Mon Calamari Shipyards/Legends|Mon Calamari Shipyards]], along with the larger ''Viscount''-class. The class also represented a move away from the alphanumeric designations which had been given to previous Mon Calamari ship-types, such as the [[MC80 Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80]] and [[MC90 Star Cruiser|MC90]]. Instead, they used a conventional class-name based on the name of the lead ship, the [[Mediator (Mediator-class)|''Mediator'']].<ref name="VP" />

The new battle cruisers were introduced during the [[Osarian-Rhommamool conflict]] prior to the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]<ref name="TCSWE" /> and saw service in the opening months of the galactic invasion. The class's namesake, the ''Mediator'', can be considered the first major combat casualty of the conflict: while she was deployed to intervene in a dispute between the planets [[Rhommamool/Legends|Rhommamool]] and [[Osarian]], enemy infiltrator [[Nom Anor]] detonated a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] laden with nuclear explosives in her hangar section, severely damaging her.<ref name="VP" />

In the first months of the war, military analysts like [[Ayddar Nylykerka]], the New Republic's head of [[New Republic Fleet Intelligence|Fleet Intelligence]], believed that the ''Mediator''-class ships already in service, when used in conjunction with [[Bothan Assault Cruiser]]s and [[Star Defender]]s, were sufficient to engage the Yuuzhan Vong successfully, and a ''Mediator''-class ship was the centerpiece of a battle group of New Republic and [[Imperial Remnant]] warships deployed at [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]] early in [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]], in anticipation of a Yuuzhan Vong strike there.<ref name="AOCI:HT">''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]''</ref>

However, during the [[Battle of Ord Mantell (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Ord Mantell]], this ship was destroyed after a vicious close-combat engagement with several enemy vessels, including a ''[[Miid ro'ik|Miid Ro'ik]]''.
Not much is known of them after this event.<ref name="AOCI:HT" />

Although Mon Calamari was believed to have been cut off before the end of the first year of the war, it appears that secret [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] were in fact kept open, and that new ships continued to be supplied to the New Republic fleets—including the ''Mediator''-class vessels to replace those lost and damaged in the opening stages of the conflict.

A number of ''Mediator''-class ships were part of the combined Alliance fleet guarding Mon Calamari and its shipyards in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]]. The ships served under [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Traest Kre'fey]] during the [[Defense of Mon Calamari (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Defense of Mon Calamari]] and were also part of the final assault of the war during the [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar]].<ref name="TUF" />

==Behind the scenes==
The size of the ''Mediator''-class battle cruiser is uncertain. A lower limit is provided in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]'', where a ship of this class is said to be "larger" than a 1.6 km ''Miid Ro'ik'', while ''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' says the Star Defender ''Viscount'' is "nearly twice the size" of ''Mediator'': The ''Viscount''{{'s}} length has subsequently been specified as 3 kilometers long in ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', which could suggest the ''Mediator'' somewhat over 1,500 meters, or alternatively not be connected as the half-length comparison was set prior when the ''Viscount'' was 17 kilometers.
